page.content: In Q1 2024, Munich Re's distribution channel mix revealed significant performance metrics across various categories. The total premiums collected were CHF115M, with claims paid amounting to CHF43M. Direct sales generated CHF45M in premiums and CHF20M in claims paid, accounting for 40% of total premiums.

lines.text: In Q1 2024, Munich Re's distribution channel mix revealed significant performance metrics across various categories. The total
premiums collected were CHF115M, with claims paid amounting to CHF43M. Direct sales generated CHF45M in premiums
and CHF20M in claims paid, accounting for 40% of total premiums.

page.content: The brokerage channel contributed CHF30M in premiums with CHF10M in claims paid, reflecting 25% of the total premiums. Online sales accounted for CHF25M in premiums and CHF5M in claims paid, representing 15% of the total premiums. Partnerships generated CHF15M in premiums with CHF8M in claims paid, making up 10% of the total. Others accounted for CHF12M in premiums and CHF10M in claims paid, also 10% of total premiums. The overall premium growth was 8%, and the claims ratio stood at 37%. The claims ratio for direct sales was 45%, while brokerage recorded a 15% ratio. Online sales had a claims ratio of 20%, and partnerships maintained a claims ratio of 10%, with others at 5%. The average premium was CHF1,000, and customer growth reached 4,500, leading to a total of 25,000 policies. Market share was recorded at 12%, with a retention rate of 90% and a net promoter score of 45. This comprehensive evaluation of the distribution channels underscores the robust performance of Munich Re in a competitive market landscape.
